article_meta.title: Consumer Published November 25, 2025 9:00am EST | Updated November 25, 2025 9:00am EST Nearly 1 in 4 American households

raw_response: {"detections":[{"biasType":"Sensationalism and Emotionalism","biasedText":"Nearly 1 in 4 American households living paycheck to paycheck, report reveals","explanation":"The headline uses 'reveals' which adds dramatic weight, implying a shocking discovery, when the data shows a modest 0.3 percentage point increase from the prior year."},{"biasType":"Slant","biasedText":"NEW CAR PRICES SOAR TO HISTORIC HIGH AS BUYERS FACE MOUNTING STICKER SHOCK","explanation":"This promotional cross-link is inserted twice within the article, reinforcing a narrative of financial distress and economic negativity beyond what the Bank of America report covers, structurally amplifying the negative framing."},{"biasType":"Negativity Bias","biasedText":"Inflation has continued to strain the budgets of American households.","explanation":"This is the author's own caption language presenting a one-sided negative characterization as fact. While the article's own data shows middle- and higher-income households have seen little to no increase in paycheck-to-paycheck living, this framing generalizes the strain to all American households."},{"biasType":"Slant","biasedText":"Nearly a quarter of all U.S. households are living paycheck to paycheck and the number has risen over the last year, though the rate of growth has slowed, according to a new report.","explanation":"The lead foregrounds the negative statistic (nearly a quarter living paycheck to paycheck, number rising) while the mitigating detail (growth rate has slowed significantly, nearly three times lower than a year ago) is subordinated to a dependent clause. The article's own data shows meaningful improvement in the trend, but the framing emphasizes the negative."}]}

detections.biasedText: Nearly 1 in 4 American households living paycheck to paycheck, report reveals
detections.explanation: The headline uses 'reveals' which adds dramatic weight, implying a shocking discovery, when the data shows a modest 0.3 percentage point increase from the prior year.

detections.biasedText: NEW CAR PRICES SOAR TO HISTORIC HIGH AS BUYERS FACE MOUNTING STICKER SHOCK
detections.explanation: This promotional cross-link is inserted twice within the article, reinforcing a narrative of financial distress and economic negativity beyond what the Bank of America report covers, structurally amplifying the negative framing.

detections.biasedText: Inflation has continued to strain the budgets of American households.
detections.explanation: This is the author's own caption language presenting a one-sided negative characterization as fact. While the article's own data shows middle- and higher-income households have seen little to no increase in paycheck-to-paycheck living, this framing generalizes the strain to all American households.

detections.biasedText: Nearly a quarter of all U.S. households are living paycheck to paycheck and the number has risen over the last year, though the rate of growth has slowed, according to a new report.
detections.explanation: The lead foregrounds the negative statistic (nearly a quarter living paycheck to paycheck, number rising) while the mitigating detail (growth rate has slowed significantly, nearly three times lower than a year ago) is subordinated to a dependent clause. The article's own data shows meaningful improvement in the trend, but the framing emphasizes the negative.
